mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are negative more than 55% of the time over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 3.5%, restrictive monetary policy increases discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5% over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal a recessionary demand contraction that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined signal of INDPRO decline and UNRATE increase does not produce negative 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when unemployment (UNRATE) is below 5%, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply overheating and impending monetary tightening, which reduces S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with capacity constraints
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when UNRATE < 5% and INDPRO is accelerating, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following RSAFS increases are not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% annualized triggers inflation-risk repricing in equities as real discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns after CPI acceleration are positive more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5%, wage pressures compress profit margins and the Fed responds with tighter policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5% over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with a falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal a credit-fueled demand recovery that boosts cyclical earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of HOUST increase and FEDFUNDS decrease fails to produce positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject.
